questions.question_text: A buyer in Michigan wants to tour homes with an agent. As of August 2024, what must occur before the first showing?
questions.correct_answer: A written buyer representation agreement must be signed
distractors: The agent must first verify the buyer's mortgage pre-approval letter
The buyer must attend an agency orientation and education class
The seller must give written approval of the buyer's agent choice
questions.explanation: Effective August 2024, Michigan buyers must sign a written buyer representation agreement before an agent can begin touring properties with them. This is a significant change from prior practice.

questions.question_text: In which Michigan agency arrangement does the brokerage assign separate agents to represent each party?
distractors: Dual agency
Transaction coordination
Single agency
questions.explanation: In designated agency, the brokerage assigns (designates) one agent to represent the seller and a different agent to represent the buyer. Each designated agent owes full fiduciary duties to their respective client.

questions.question_text: A Michigan transaction coordinator is helping both parties complete paperwork. What duties does the coordinator owe?
questions.correct_answer: Honesty, fair dealing, and material fact disclosure
distractors: Full fiduciary duties to both parties
Fiduciary duties only to the party who pays
No duties beyond completing paperwork
questions.explanation: A transaction coordinator in Michigan does not represent either party and therefore does not owe fiduciary duties. They owe honesty, fair dealing, and a duty to disclose material facts to all parties.

questions.question_text: A buyer contacts a listing agent directly and wants to write an offer without their own agent. Which role options are available to the listing agent?
questions.correct_answer: Seller agent, dual agent, or transaction coordinator
distractors: Buyer agent or seller agent are the only options
Dual agent is the only permitted role available
The listing agent must refuse to work with the buyer
questions.explanation: The listing agent can remain as seller's agent only, become a dual agent with both parties' consent, or act as a transaction coordinator. They cannot become the buyer's agent while still representing the seller.

questions.question_text: What distinguishes a buyer agent from a transaction coordinator in Michigan?
questions.correct_answer: A buyer agent owes fiduciary duties to the buyer
distractors: A buyer agent cannot show listed properties
A transaction coordinator charges higher fees
There is no legal distinction between them
questions.explanation: A buyer agent has a fiduciary relationship with the buyer including loyalty, confidentiality, and advocacy. A transaction coordinator merely facilitates the transaction without representing either party.

questions.question_text: When must a Michigan real estate agent first provide a written agency disclosure?
questions.correct_answer: At first substantive contact with a party
distractors: When the purchase agreement is signed
When the listing agreement is executed
Within 3 days of showing a property
questions.explanation: Michigan requires written agency disclosure at the first substantive contact with a party. Substantive contact means discussing specific property details, pricing, or personal motivation.

questions.question_text: Agent Ross meets a prospective buyer at an open house who asks about the home's price reduction history. What must Ross do?
questions.correct_answer: Provide agency disclosure before discussing details
distractors: Answer freely since open houses are exempt
Wait until the buyer submits a written offer
Only disclose if the buyer requests agent representation
questions.explanation: Asking about price reduction history is substantive contact. Ross must provide the written agency disclosure form before engaging in that discussion, even at an open house.

questions.question_text: What information must Michigan's agency disclosure form identify?
questions.correct_answer: The type of agency relationship offered
distractors: The agent's commission percentage
The property's current listing price
The agent's years of experience
questions.explanation: The Michigan agency disclosure form must identify the type of agency relationship (seller agent, buyer agent, dual agent, etc.) so parties can make informed decisions about representation.

questions.question_text: A buyer refuses to sign the agency disclosure acknowledgment form. What should the Michigan agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Note the refusal on the form and proceed
distractors: Refuse to work with the buyer entirely
Have the buyer's attorney sign instead
Skip the disclosure requirement for this buyer
questions.explanation: If a party refuses to acknowledge the disclosure, the agent should note the refusal on the form. The agent has fulfilled their obligation by presenting the disclosure, and the refusal to sign does not void the requirement.

questions.question_text: Agent Kim provides an agency disclosure form to the seller at listing. Later, a buyer submits an offer through Kim's brokerage. Who else must receive the disclosure?
questions.correct_answer: The buyer must also acknowledge the disclosure
distractors: No one, the seller's disclosure covers both parties
Only the buyer's attorney needs notification
The mortgage lender must receive a copy
questions.explanation: All parties in the transaction must acknowledge the agency disclosure. The seller's acknowledgment does not cover the buyer. Each party must understand the agency relationships involved.

questions.question_text: Which set of duties does a Michigan buyer's agent owe to the client?
questions.correct_answer: Loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, accounting, care
distractors: Honesty, fair dealing, and material fact disclosure only
Disclosure of known material facts to all parties only
Obedience, accounting, and fair dealing to all parties only
questions.explanation: A Michigan agent acting as a buyer's or seller's agent owes the full set of fiduciary duties: loyalty, obedience, disclosure, confidentiality, accounting, and reasonable care (often remembered as OLDCAR).

questions.question_text: Seller's agent Taylor learns the property has a cracked foundation. The seller instructs Taylor not to tell buyers. What should Taylor do?
questions.correct_answer: Disclose it because material facts must be revealed
distractors: Obey the seller's instruction to remain silent
Only disclose if the buyer asks about it directly
Withdraw from the listing without disclosing it
questions.explanation: While agents owe obedience to clients, the duty of disclosure of material facts overrides. A cracked foundation is a material defect that must be disclosed regardless of the seller's wishes.

questions.question_text: A buyer's agent learns that her client plans to flip the property. A competing buyer asks the agent about her client's intentions. What should the agent do?
questions.correct_answer: Decline to share confidential client information
distractors: Share the information to ensure fair dealing
Disclose it as a material fact of the transaction
Report the buyer's plans to the listing agent
questions.explanation: A buyer's agent owes confidentiality to the client. The buyer's investment plans are confidential information that must not be shared with competing parties or their agents.

questions.question_text: Agent Patel acts as a transaction coordinator. A buyer asks Patel to negotiate aggressively on their behalf. How should Patel respond?
questions.correct_answer: Explain that a coordinator cannot advocate for either party
distractors: Negotiate as requested since the buyer asked for it
Refer all negotiation matters to the listing agent
Charge an additional separate fee for negotiation
questions.explanation: Transaction coordinators do not represent either party and cannot advocate or negotiate on behalf of one side. Patel should explain this limitation and suggest the buyer obtain their own representation.

questions.question_text: Which duty do both fiduciary agents and transaction coordinators owe to the parties in Michigan?
distractors: Loyalty to both parties in the transaction
Obedience to all client instructions given
Confidentiality of all negotiating positions
questions.explanation: Both fiduciary agents and transaction coordinators must disclose known material facts. However, loyalty, obedience, and confidentiality are fiduciary duties owed only by agents to their clients, not by transaction coordinators.
